other_observers: observed with my brother Darren Dowell; bird found two days earlier by Glenn Walbek and Joe Roller
description: The Arctic Tern was flying over the western part of the lake and diving occasionally.  The acrobatic flight and rapid wingbeat rate indicated that it was a small/medium-sized tern.  Through the scope, we could see that it had a complete black cap and a short bill.  The back and upperside of the wings were mostly a uniform pale gray color, and there was a narrow, white trailing bar on the secondaries.  A review of our photographs revealed that the bill was red, and that there was thin black edging on the wing tips.
photographer: Photographer: David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell, David Dowell
